chunk_summary: This section emphasizes the importance of anchoring malnutrition protocols to specific guideline sources and years to avoid discrepancies in screening, classification, and treatment workflows.
learning_objectives: Understand the risks of mixing malnutrition thresholds and criteria from different guidelines.
Learn to align local protocols with authoritative sources and year-specific editions.
Identify key guideline sources for malnutrition management in India.
Apply conservative decision-making when protocol conflicts arise.
key_facts: WHO guideline (Dec 2023) provides global definitions and an expanded infant-under-six-month 'at risk' framework.
MoHFW Operational Guidelines (May 2011) define inpatient criteria, shock management, and stabilization steps.
ICMR Standard Treatment Workflow (Oct 2019) includes antibiotic escalation pathways and investigation bundles.
IMNCI materials (2023) guide danger sign recognition and first-contact triage classification.
NTEP pediatric TB guidance (2022) standardizes when thinness or poor weight gain should trigger TB evaluation.
reference_quote: A practical India-oriented stamp set is: the WHO guideline on prevention and management of wasting and nutritional oedema in under-fives (Dec 2023) for global definitions and the expanded infant-under-six-month 'at risk' framework.
